title: Dan Dan-Style Peanut Noodles

ingredients.name: dried wheat noodles (hakka or thin egg noodles)

subs.name: any flat wheat noodles or even spaghetti
subs.notes: spaghetti is a surprisingly good substitute for Chinese wheat noodles

ingredients.name: minced chicken or pork
visual_checks: forms the savoury topping; can be omitted for a vegetarian version
ingredients.notes: forms the savoury topping; can be omitted for a vegetarian version

subs.name: finely diced mushroom + fried until crispy, for a vegetarian version
subs.notes: shiitake or button mushrooms work well; ensure they are browned properly

ingredients.name: roasted peanuts or peanut butter
visual_checks: blend peanuts with 2 tbsp water to make a coarse paste, or use 3 tbsp peanut butter
ingredients.notes: blend peanuts with 2 tbsp water to make a coarse paste, or use 3 tbsp peanut butter

subs.name: 3 tbsp tahini (sesame paste)
subs.notes: gives a different but equally rich nutty base

ingredients.name: chilli oil or chilli-garlic sauce
visual_checks: use more or less to control heat
ingredients.notes: use more or less to control heat

subs.name: 1 tsp red chilli powder + 1 tsp oil heated together
subs.notes: homemade chilli oil: heat oil to smoking, pour over chilli powder in a heatproof bowl

ingredients.name: Sichuan peppercorns or black pepper, ground
spring onions, thinly sliced, for garnish

steps.text: Cook noodles in boiling salted water according to packet instructions until just tender. Drain, rinse briefly with cold water and toss with 1 teaspoon of sesame oil to prevent sticking.

Make the sauce: in a bowl, whisk together the peanut paste (or peanut butter), soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, chilli sauce, remaining sesame oil and ground pepper. Thin with 3–4 tablespoons of the noodle cooking water until the sauce is pourable but still thick. Taste and adjust heat and salt.

Heat oil in a wok or kadai over high heat. Add garlic and ginger; stir for 30 seconds. Add the minced meat and break it up, stir-frying on high heat for 4–5 minutes until the meat is browned and crispy at the edges — this textural contrast is important.
Season the cooked meat with a splash of soy sauce and remove from heat.
Divide the noodles between bowls. Pour the peanut sauce generously over each portion and spoon the crispy meat on top. Finish with sliced spring onions.
Serve immediately and toss everything together at the table before eating — the contrast of cold noodles, warm sauce and crispy meat should all be experienced together.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: peanut → 3 tbsp tahini (sesame paste) (gives a different but equally rich nutty base); keema → finely diced mushroom + fried until crispy, for a vegetarian version (shiitake or button mushrooms work well; ensure they are browned properly); green-chilli-sauce → 1 tsp red chilli powder + 1 tsp oil heated together (homemade chilli oil: heat oil to smoking, pour over chilli powder in a heatproof bowl); noodles → any flat wheat noodles or even spaghetti (spaghetti is a surprisingly good substitute for Chinese wheat noodles)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Verkadalai (groundnuts) are your peanuts — dry-roast them on a tawa and blend coarsely. Hakka noodles (Chings brand) are perfect and widely available. Gingelly oil (nallennai) is your sesame oil. Chilli-garlic sauce from Chings or Mother's Recipe works as chilli oil. The whole dish can be made in a kadai and a saucepan in under 30 minutes.
